title: Mini Shai-Hulud Supply Chain Worm Targets SAP npm Packages Exfiltrating Credentials to ICP Canister and GitHub Repos
description: The Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain worm targeted SAP-related npm packages in April 2026, collecting tokens, credentials, API and SSH keys, cloud service secrets, CI/CD system tokens, Kubernetes and Docker configurations, and LLM platform credentials before exfiltrating to both a conventional webhook and an ICP canister endpoint with hardcoded canister ID cjn37-uyaaa-aaaac-qgnva-cai.
label: AV-410

title: Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Steals MetaMask and Phantom Browser Extension Data Plus Solana Ethereum Bitcoin Exodus Wallet Files
description: The Mini Shai-Hulud npm worm contained dedicated logic to extract browser extension data associated with MetaMask and Phantom cryptocurrency wallets, alongside local cryptocurrency wallet files including Solana, Ethereum, Bitcoin, Exodus, and Atomic Wallet data, targeting the full spectrum of cryptocurrency assets accessible from compromised developer systems.
label: AV-410

title: PyTorch Lightning PyPI Versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3 Compromised as Mini Shai-Hulud Extension Bypassing Source Control
description: PyTorch Lightning versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3 were compromised on April 30 2026 when an attacker with stolen PyPI credentials cloned the open source code, injected a malicious payload into a hidden _runtime directory containing a downloader and obfuscated JavaScript payload, and pushed tampered builds directly to PyPI bypassing source control entirely.
label: AV-400

title: Lightning PyPI Compromise Downloads Bun JavaScript Runtime to Execute 11MB Obfuscated Credential-Stealing Payload
description: The compromised PyTorch Lightning PyPI package initiated an attack chain through a Python start.py script that downloaded and executed the Bun JavaScript runtime, then used it to run an 11MB obfuscated malicious payload router_runtime.js designed to conduct comprehensive credential theft from developer and CI/CD environments.
label: AV-400

title: Mini Shai-Hulud Writes Malicious Payload to .claude/settings.json and .vscode/tasks.json for Persistent IDE-Level Execution
description: The Mini Shai-Hulud malware payload in the Lightning PyPI compromise wrote malicious payload files to .claude/settings.json and .vscode/tasks.json paths, establishing persistence at the IDE configuration level to ensure malicious code executed automatically whenever developers opened their AI coding assistant or VS Code editor.
label: AV-410

title: Mini Shai-Hulud Creates Public GitHub Repository Titled A Mini Shai-Hulud has Appeared as Fallback Exfiltration Channel
description: The Mini Shai-Hulud malware used a distinctive public GitHub repository titled A Mini Shai-Hulud has Appeared as a fallback credential exfiltration channel when primary ICP canister exfiltration failed, using stolen GitHub tokens to create and write encrypted credential payloads to the public repository as encrypted data blobs.
label: AV-410

title: QLNX Threat Actor Deploys Full Linux Implant with PAM Hooking to Steal npm PyPI AWS Kubernetes and Docker Hub Credentials
description: The QLNX threat actor deployed a full-fledged Linux implant combining remote access, stealth, persistence, and credential harvesting in a single payload with minimal detection footprint, using PAM hooking to capture authentication credentials alongside stealing npm, PyPI, AWS, Kubernetes, Docker Hub, environment variables, authentication tokens, and plaintext passwords from compromised developer systems.
label: AV-400

title: QLNX Linux Implant Creates Backdoors in Production Environments via Trojanized Package Updates Reaching Millions of Users
description: The QLNX Linux implant established backdoors in production environments through trojanized package updates, with the operational impact including potential malicious updates delivered to millions of end users, persistent backdoors in production cloud infrastructure, and unauthorized access to developer workstations and CI/CD pipeline systems.
label: AV-400

title: TeamPCP Conducts Second Supply Chain Attack Against Checkmarx npm Packages Within Two Months of Initial Compromise
description: The TeamPCP threat group conducted a second supply chain attack against Checkmarx npm packages within two months of their initial compromise, demonstrating the persistence and targeting focus of sophisticated supply chain attack groups that return to the same victims with refined techniques after initial detection and remediation.
label: AV-200

title: CanisterWorm Attack Linked to TeamPCP Group Uses Identical ICP Canister Infrastructure to CanisterSprawl Campaign
description: Socket researchers linked CanisterWorm attacks to the TeamPCP threat group based on identical ICP canister command-and-control infrastructure, credential theft methods, and self-propagation techniques observed in the earlier CanisterSprawl campaign, establishing TeamPCP as a persistent multi-campaign supply chain threat actor.
label: AV-410

title: npm Supply Chain Worm Jumps to PyPI Ecosystem Using .pth-Based Payload When Python Credentials Are Found on Victim System
description: A new npm supply chain worm documented by Socket and StepSecurity applied a .pth-based payload to Python packages when PyPI credentials were found on victim developer systems, making this a multi-ecosystem attack that automatically jumped from the JavaScript to Python package ecosystem using whatever credentials were available on the compromised machine.
label: AV-509

title: Socket Advises Auditing npm Packages for Same public.pem File Webhook Host or postinstall Pattern as IoC for Worm Infection
description: Socket security researchers advised organizations to audit their npm environments for indicators of compromise including packages sharing the same public.pem file, identical webhook host endpoints, or matching postinstall execution patterns as reliable indicators that additional npm packages may be infected with the same supply chain worm payload.
label: AV-410

title: intercom-client PyPI Package Compromised Alongside Lightning in Coordinated Mini Shai-Hulud Supply Chain Campaign
description: The intercom-client PyPI package was compromised alongside PyTorch Lightning versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3 in a coordinated Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain campaign, demonstrating that the April 2026 attack targeted multiple high-value Python packages simultaneously to maximize credential theft reach across the Python developer ecosystem.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in .env Environment Configuration Files Alter Variable Names and Secret Values
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in .env environment configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter environment variable names and values, causing applications to read different secret values and configuration parameters than what developers see when reviewing environment files during security audits and code reviews.
label: AV-300

title: PyPI Suspends New User Registrations and Package Uploads in May 2023 Due to Significant Increase in Malicious Package Activity
description: PyPI suspended new user registrations and package uploads in May 2023 in response to a significant increase in malicious package submissions, demonstrating that supply chain attacks on centralized registries had reached a scale requiring emergency measures to protect the Python developer community from automated malicious package campaigns.
label: AV-200

title: Hitchhiker Guide Research Documents Arbitrary Code Execution via Malicious Package Distribution with Evasion Techniques
description: Academic research titled The Hitchhiker Guide to Malicious Third-Party Dependencies documented how attackers achieve Arbitrary Code Execution through distribution of malicious packages, analyzing evasion techniques including delayed execution, environment detection, and obfuscation that allow malicious packages to bypass automated security scanning in package registries.
label: AV-400

title: Open Source Software Comprising Up to 98 Percent of Modern Codebases Creates Systemic Supply Chain Attack Risk for Global Infrastructure
description: Academic research citing Linux Foundation data documented that open source software accounts for up to 98 percent of modern application codebases, establishing that supply chain attacks on widely-used open source packages represent a systemic risk to global software infrastructure requiring national security-level attention and coordinated defensive response.
label: AV-509

title: Mini Shai-Hulud Targets SAP Enterprise npm Packages to Access Enterprise Resource Planning System Credentials and Cloud Infrastructure
description: The Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain worm specifically targeted SAP-related npm packages used in enterprise resource planning system integrations, seeking to harvest credentials for SAP cloud services, enterprise APIs, and connected business infrastructure that would provide access to sensitive financial and operational data in large enterprise organizations.
label: AV-410

title: pgserve CanisterSprawl Worm Bumps Patch Version of Every Package Victim Can Publish to Disguise Malicious Republication
description: The pgserve CanisterSprawl worm used a sophisticated version bumping strategy where for each npm package the victim had publish access to, it incremented the patch version number before injecting the malicious payload and republishing, making compromised versions appear as legitimate routine maintenance updates to both developers and automated dependency management tools.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Prisma Database Schema Files Alter Model Definitions and Access Control Relations
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Prisma database schema files where hidden Unicode characters alter data model field definitions and relation configurations, causing generated Prisma client code to expose different data access patterns and bypass row-level security controls from what database engineers intend during schema design review.
label: AV-300

title: Shai-Hulud and Mini Shai-Hulud Extract Secrets from GitHub Actions Runner Worker Memory Using Advanced Memory Scanning Techniques
description: Both Shai-Hulud and Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain worms implemented advanced techniques to extract secrets from GitHub Actions Runner Worker process memory, scanning process memory spaces for plaintext credential patterns including API keys, tokens, and passwords that CI/CD systems temporarily hold in memory during workflow execution.
label: AV-410

title: Mini Shai-Hulud PHP Payload Mirrors Broader Tradecraft Including Install-Time Execution Bun Runtime and Heavily Obfuscated JavaScript
description: The Mini Shai-Hulud PHP payload found in the Lightning and SAP npm compromises mirrored the broader Mini Shai-Hulud tradecraft across recent npm and PyPI compromises including install-time execution, Bun-based payload launch, heavily obfuscated JavaScript, and credential harvesting from developer and CI/CD environments with encrypted exfiltration.
label: AV-400

title: Namastex.ai npm Packages Linked to CanisterSprawl via Identical Install-Time Execution Credential Theft and ICP Canister Exfiltration
description: Socket investigation linked compromised Namastex.ai npm packages to the CanisterSprawl campaign based on identical core methods including install-time execution, credential theft targeting developer environment secrets, off-host exfiltration to canister-backed ICP infrastructure, and self-propagation logic that infected additional npm packages using stolen publish tokens.
label: AV-200

title: Supply Chain Malware Specifically Targets Kubernetes Service Account Tokens and kubeconfig Files for Container Orchestration Access
description: Multiple supply chain attack campaigns documented in 2025 and 2026 specifically targeted Kubernetes service account tokens and kubeconfig configuration files, providing attackers with access to container orchestration infrastructure used to run production workloads, enabling lateral movement into Kubernetes clusters hosting sensitive applications.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Liquibase Database Migration Scripts Alter SQL Statements and Schema Change Definitions
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Liquibase database migration scripts where hidden Unicode characters alter SQL statement content and schema change definitions, causing database migration tools to execute different SQL operations from what database administrators and developers review in changeset files.
label: AV-300

title: Shai-Hulud Worm Clones All Private Repositories to Attacker-Controlled Accounts Using Stolen GitHub Access Tokens
description: The Shai-Hulud worm used stolen GitHub access tokens to authenticate to the GitHub API, identify all repositories including private ones accessible to the compromised account, clone private repositories to attacker-controlled accounts, and create new branches with malicious workflows to automate ongoing data theft from the victim organization.
label: AV-410

title: Automated Bot Accounts Create Thousands of PyPI Maintainer Accounts to Upload Malicious Packages at Industrial Scale
description: Security researchers documented automated bot account creation campaigns that registered thousands of PyPI maintainer accounts to upload malicious packages at industrial scale, forcing PyPI to implement email verification, CAPTCHA challenges, and new account rate limiting to combat automated malicious package registration campaigns.
label: AV-200

title: QLNX Linux Implant Hooks PAM Authentication System to Capture All Passwords Entered on Compromised Developer Systems
description: The QLNX Linux implant deployed by supply chain attacks hooked the Pluggable Authentication Module system to intercept and capture all passwords entered on compromised developer machines including sudo authentication, SSH login passwords, and application authentication, providing comprehensive credential harvesting beyond stored credential files.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Flyway Database Migration SQL Files Execute Different DML Statements Than Reviewed Source
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Flyway database migration SQL files where hidden Unicode characters alter Data Manipulation Language statement logic, causing Flyway to execute INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations that differ from what database reviewers approve during migration script code review processes.
label: AV-300

title: Docker Hub Official Image Namespace Squatting Causes Enterprise CI Systems to Pull Attacker-Controlled Base Container Images
description: Security researchers documented Docker Hub namespace squatting attacks targeting the official image namespace where attackers registered image names closely resembling official Docker Hub images before legitimate projects could claim their namespace, causing enterprise CI/CD systems to pull attacker-controlled base container images during automated build processes.
label: AV-509

title: Shai-Hulud Recursive Threat Vector Remains Latent Risk as Dependencies Update Across All Packages That Imported Compromised Versions
description: Trend Micro documented that Shai-Hulud was engineered for persistence as a recursive threat vector that remained a latent risk as dependencies updated, ensuring that all packages which had imported compromised versions would continue spreading the worm through the npm ecosystem as developers updated their dependencies.
label: AV-410

title: QLNX Attack Demonstrates Limitations of Uniform Security Controls Applied to Developer Environments With Elevated Trust Requirements
description: The QLNX supply chain attack campaign highlighted fundamental limitations of applying uniform security controls to developer environments, where the elevated trust and broad access granted to development tools and package managers creates security trade-offs that make traditional endpoint protection and network monitoring insufficient for detecting supply chain compromises.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Alembic Database Migration Scripts Alter Column Definitions and Index Configurations
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Alembic Python database migration scripts where hidden Unicode characters alter column type definitions, constraint specifications, and index configurations, causing SQLAlchemy-based applications to create database schemas with different structure and security properties from what developers intend.
label: AV-300

title: Shai-Hulud Worm Communicates with Remote Servers to Receive Updates Allowing Real-Time Evolution After Initial Deployment
description: Trend Micro documented that Shai-Hulud maintained network communication with remote command-and-control servers to exfiltrate data and receive payload updates, enabling the worm to evolve its capabilities and evasion techniques in real-time after initial deployment without requiring a new infection vector.
label: AV-400

title: Supply Chain Worms Exfiltrate Stolen Credentials as Encrypted Payloads to Public GitHub Repositories for Resilient Data Retrieval
description: Multiple supply chain worm campaigns documented by Wiz researchers exfiltrated stolen credentials and secrets as encrypted payloads committed to public GitHub repositories, providing attackers with a resilient and globally accessible credential storage mechanism that uses GitHub infrastructure and is extremely difficult to take down or block.
label: AV-509

title: PyPI Administrators Quarantine Lightning Project After Coordinated Detection by Aikido Security OX Security Socket and StepSecurity
description: PyPI administrators quarantined the Lightning project after coordinated discovery and reporting by Aikido Security, OX Security, Socket, and StepSecurity, demonstrating that effective supply chain attack response requires multi-vendor security research collaboration to detect, analyze, and remediate compromised packages before widespread installation.
label: AV-410

title: Supply Chain Malware Targets LLM Platform API Keys as High-Value New Credential Category for AI Service Access and Abuse
description: Security researchers documented that 2025 and 2026 supply chain attack campaigns began specifically targeting LLM platform API keys for services including OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, and Amazon Bedrock as a high-value new credential category, enabling attackers to abuse expensive AI compute resources at victim expense.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in package.json Scripts Section Alter npm Lifecycle Command Execution
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in npm package.json scripts sections where hidden Unicode characters alter lifecycle hook commands, causing npm to execute different shell commands during install, build, test, and start lifecycle events from what package maintainers and security auditors see during package.json review.
label: AV-300

title: Wiz Documents Second-Stage Supply Chain Payload as Credential Stealer and Propagation Framework Targeting GitHub npm and Cloud Secrets
description: Wiz security researchers documented a second-stage supply chain payload functioning as a combined credential stealer and propagation framework that collected GitHub tokens, npm credentials, cloud secrets for AWS Azure and GCP, Kubernetes tokens, and GitHub Actions secrets using advanced techniques including extracting secrets from runner memory before exfiltrating via public GitHub repositories.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud Initial Infection via Phishing Email Disguised as Official npm Security Alert Tricking Developer into Revealing Credentials
description: The Shai-Hulud attack chain began with a phishing email carefully disguised as an official npm security alert notification, tricking a target developer into clicking a link and revealing their npm account credentials, demonstrating that social engineering against individual developers remains the primary initial access vector for ecosystem-wide supply chain attacks.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Dockerfile ENTRYPOINT and CMD Instructions Alter Container Startup Command Execution
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Dockerfile ENTRYPOINT and CMD instructions where hidden Unicode characters alter the container startup commands and arguments, causing Docker containers to execute different processes from what container engineers review during Dockerfile security inspection and approval.
label: AV-300

title: QLNX Attack Demonstrates Persistence of Supply Chain Attacks Due to Limitations of Traditional Signature-Based Detection Systems
description: The QLNX supply chain attack campaigns demonstrated the fundamental limitations of traditional signature-based security detection for supply chain threats, where novel malware families and obfuscated payloads consistently evade antivirus and endpoint detection systems that rely on known malware signatures rather than behavioral analysis.
label: AV-509

title: GitGuardian Documents Coordinated Simultaneous Supply Chain Attacks Hitting npm PyPI and Docker Hub Within 48 Hours
description: GitGuardian documented a coordinated supply chain attack campaign that simultaneously targeted npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub ecosystems within a 48-hour window, with the TeamPCP threat group linked to two of the three campaigns, demonstrating the increasing sophistication and multi-ecosystem coordination of supply chain attack operations.
label: AV-200

title: Shai-Hulud Embeds Unix Shell Scripts in npm postinstall to Establish Persistence and Begin Credential Exfiltration on Installation
description: The Shai-Hulud worm embedded Unix shell scripts alongside JavaScript in npm postinstall hooks, using the shell scripts to establish system-level persistence mechanisms and initiate credential exfiltration immediately upon package installation, combining JavaScript and shell scripting for cross-platform compatibility and persistence establishment.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in nginx Location Block Definitions Alter URL Pattern Matching and Proxy Pass Routing
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in nginx location block definitions where hidden Unicode characters alter URL pattern matching expressions and proxy_pass routing rules, causing nginx web servers to route requests to different backend services from what administrators see when reviewing reverse proxy configurations.
label: AV-300

title: npm Supply Chain Worms Achieve Secondary and Tertiary Infection Waves via postinstall Self-Replication into Developer Dependencies
description: Trend Micro documented that npm supply chain worms engineered for self-replication achieve secondary and tertiary infection waves by using compromised developer npm tokens to inject malicious payloads into every package the victim can publish, creating expanding circles of infection that continue propagating as downstream developers install newly infected packages.
label: AV-400

title: termncolor Threat Actor Uses Zulip Messaging Platform as Command-and-Control Channel Active Since July 2025 with 90692 Messages
description: Further analysis of the termncolor supply chain attack revealed the threat actor used Zulip as a command-and-control messaging platform with three active users exchanging 90,692 messages within the organization since July 10 2025, providing security researchers with insight into the operational scale and communication infrastructure of the supply chain attack campaign.
label: AV-200

title: Container Image Layer Cache Poisoning Injects Malicious Code Between Multi-Stage Build Stages in Enterprise CI Pipelines
description: Security researchers documented container image layer cache poisoning attacks targeting multi-stage Docker builds where malicious code is injected into intermediate build layers that are cached and reused across pipeline runs, contaminating all subsequent builds that use the poisoned cache layers including production release artifacts.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Gradle Kotlin DSL Build Scripts Alter Dependency Versions and Plugin Configuration
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Gradle Kotlin DSL build scripts where hidden Unicode characters alter dependency version specifications and plugin configuration parameters, causing Android and JVM build systems to compile applications with different library versions and build configurations from what engineers see during Gradle script review.
label: AV-300

title: Supply Chain Worms Enumerate All Accessible Repositories via GitHub API affiliation Parameter to Maximize Infection Propagation
description: Trend Micro documented that supply chain worms issued GitHub API requests to /user/repos with affiliation=owner,collaborator,organization_member to enumerate all repositories accessible to compromised accounts including private organizational repositories, identifying every location where the worm could inject malicious workflows to automate further credential theft and propagation.
label: AV-410
